title: Embargoed race-level CV plus fold-safe logit blending with beta calibration
summary: Adjacent laps from the same driver-race have overlapping label horizons, so naive grouped CV can still overstate quality.
rationale: Adjacent laps from the same driver-race have overlapping label horizons, so naive grouped CV can still overstate quality. Separately, beta calibration is a lightweight post-processing method that often behaves better than isotonic when scores are skewed; applied on OOF logits it can stabilize blends even when AUC itself is unchanged by monotone calibration.
implementation_hint: Evaluate models with Race_Year holdout or grouped time splits plus a 1-lap embargo around test windows for the same Driver x Race sequence. Train two complementary models, generate OOF logits, fit per-regime beta calibrators or a simple logistic stacker on OOF only, then blend for submission.
expected_effect: Better model selection discipline and a more leaderboard-stable ensemble; it may not lift raw CV much, but can improve generalization and reduce public/private drift.
risk: Embargoed CV usually lowers local scores, making progress look worse before it gets more honest; calibration alone will not improve AUC unless used inside selection or blending.
